content:
# Anisotropy

**Summary**: The property of a variable having different properties (like range or sill) in different directions.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] identified geometric and zonal anisotropies using semivariogram analysis.

- [[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996]] evaluated the anisotropy of marker surfaces and well-log porosity.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] analyzed anisotropy to understand the directional behavior of the shale.

- [[2015-own|Own, 2015]] analyzed anisotropy to improve the accuracy of microseismic hypocenter locations.

- [[2016-hassan|Hassan, 2016]] analyzed anisotropy to identify fracture-induced variations in the AVOA gradient.

- [[2024-makki|Makki, 2024]] investigated anisotropy in sand dunes to understand its impact on seismic imaging.

## Scientific Context

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] Determining anisotropy is crucial for defining the search ellipsoid in kriging.

- [[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996]] evaluated the anisotropy of marker surfaces and well-log porosity.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] analyzed anisotropy to understand the directional behavior of the shale.

- [[2015-own|Own, 2015]] analyzed anisotropy to improve the accuracy of microseismic hypocenter locations.

- [[2016-hassan|Hassan, 2016]] analyzed anisotropy to identify fracture-induced variations in the AVOA gradient.

- [[2024-makki|Makki, 2024]] investigated anisotropy in sand dunes to understand its impact on seismic imaging.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996]] Comparing semivariograms along different directions.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Horizon 1 showed strong geometric anisotropy with an anisotropy ratio of 4. ([[1996-jarad|Jarad, 1996, p. 113]])

- Marker surface semivariograms showed marked anisotropy along the northeast/southwest direction (N 50° E). ([[1996-al-salem|Al-Salem, 1996, p. 120]])

- Analyzed Transversely Isotropic (TI) properties for the Qusaiba Shale. ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 97]])

- Anisotropic models (VTI, HTI, TTI) significantly improved perforation shot location accuracy compared to isotropic models. ([[2015-own|Own, 2015, p. 95]])

- Anisotropy was found to be very weak in both reservoirs, with an ellipticity ratio of less than 1. ([[2016-hassan|Hassan, 2016, p. 12]])

- The study confirmed a weakly anisotropic TTI medium with epsilon = 0.035 and delta = 0.005. ([[2024-makki|Makki, 2024, p. 13]])
